threats.title: VeToken Delegation Hijacking via Unrestricted stake() Receiver Parameter
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentVeToken.stake() allows any caller to specify an arbitrary `receiver` for minted veTokens and forcibly overrides that receiver's entire delegation to a caller-chosen `delegatee`. An attacker can call stake(1, victim, attackerValidator) to redirect ALL of victim's accumulated voting power to their controlled validator, at the cost of only 1 LP token.
threats.investigation_rationale: The _delegate(receiver, delegatee) call operates on the FULL balance of receiver, not just the newly minted amount. This enables wholesale governance takeover for minimal cost.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _delegate(receiver, delegatee) called with user-supplied receiver without verifying msg.sender == receiver; overrides entire balance delegation

preconditions: canStake == true (public agent) OR totalSupply == 0
Attacker holds >= 1 LP token (the staking asset)
Victim has existing veToken balance with delegation to a different validator
Agent is not blacklisted

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _delegate(receiver, delegatee) in stake() overrides delegation for receiver's ENTIRE balance, not just the new amount
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls stake(1, victim, attackerDelegatee) to redirect victim's full voting power
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker gains victim's entire governance voting power for 1 LP token; can control DAO proposals, set DAO address, change agent metadata

economic_questions: What is the total staked value delegable per agent?
Can the attacker cascade this across multiple victims to gain DAO supermajority?
Can the attacker use DAO control to call setDAO and permanently hijack governance?
validation_requirements: Confirm _delegate overwrites entire balance delegation via OZ ERC20VotesUpgradeable
Test atomic attack: stake(1, victim, attacker) in single tx
Verify no hidden check on receiver == msg.sender in _update or _delegate

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Full governance control over agent worth potentially millions in token/LP value

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Attacker needs minimal LP tokens obtainable from Uniswap
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time after victim has staked and delegated
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: No market impact; pure governance manipulation

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any veToken holder with existing delegation
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All delegated governance power per agent

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm voting power transfer is immediate and total

threats.title: AgentFactoryV4 Complete Asset Drain via assetToken as Custom Token
threats.hypothesis_description: initFromToken allows passing assetToken (e.g., VIRTUAL) as the tokenAddr. On withdraw, the factory first transfers withdrawableAmount of assetToken, then transfers the ENTIRE remaining assetToken balance (via IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this))). Since customToken == assetToken, this drains all assetToken from the factory, stealing deposits from all other active applications.
threats.investigation_rationale: There is no check preventing tokenAddr == assetToken. The isCompatibleToken check only verifies ERC20 interface compliance. The withdraw function sends the full balance of the custom token, not the tracked deposit amount.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw transfers IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this)) without checking customToken != assetToken; initFromToken has no guard against tokenAddr == assetToken

preconditions: Factory is not paused
Attacker has applicationThreshold + 1 wei of assetToken
assetToken implements standard ERC20 (name/symbol/totalSupply/balanceOf)
_tokenApplication[assetToken] == 0 (assetToken not previously registered)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: withdraw sends IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this)) which is the ENTIRE factory balance when customToken == assetToken
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Call initFromToken(assetToken, ..., 1), then withdraw(id) in separate tx after proposalEndBlock
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Net profit = factory's entire pre-existing assetToken balance (all other applications' deposits)

economic_questions: How much assetToken does the factory typically hold from active applications?
Can multiple attackers race to exploit this?
Does block.number > proposalEndBlock pass immediately since proposalEndBlock = block.number?
validation_requirements: Confirm assetToken passes isCompatibleToken check
Test full initFromToken -> withdraw flow with tokenAddr = assetToken
Verify proposalEndBlock timing allows same-block or next-block withdrawal

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Entire factory assetToken balance (potentially hundreds of thousands of USD)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Effectively infinite ROI since capital is returned

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When factory holds maximum active application deposits
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Direct theft with no market manipulation needed

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Sum of all active application deposits in the factory

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: applicationThreshold value and number of active applications
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test that withdraw(proposalEndBlock == block.number) is exploitable next block

threats.title: Permissionless Validator Registration with Inflated Base Score
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentNftV2.addValidator() is public with no access control. Anyone can register any address as a validator for any agent. _initValidatorScore grants the new validator a base score equal to totalProposals(virtualId) at registration time. Late-registering validators get free score equal to all past proposals without any actual participation.
threats.investigation_rationale: Validators who register after many proposals get an unfairly high base score, corrupting governance scoring and any reward distribution based on validator scores.

proof_of_signal.pattern: addValidator has no access control modifier; calls _addValidator and _initValidatorScore which grants baseScore = totalProposals

preconditions: Target agent exists (has been minted)
Attacker address is not already a validator for that agent

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Register after N proposals to get base score N for free; inflate totalUptimeScore
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker gets unearned validator score, potentially capturing validator rewards or manipulating ELO ratings

economic_questions: Are validator scores used for reward distribution?
Can inflated scores affect ELO calculations?
Can this combined with ECON-H001 give full governance control?
validation_requirements: Confirm addValidator has no access modifier
Verify _initValidatorScore grants totalProposals as base score
Test that registering multiple addresses inflates totalUptimeScore

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on reward distribution mechanisms using validator scores
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High for governance influence; variable for financial gain

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After agent has accumulated many proposals (higher base score)
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Governance corruption, no direct market impact

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Legitimate validators whose scores are diluted
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Governance integrity and validator reward pool

threats.title: Gas-Based DoS on totalUptimeScore via Mass Validator Registration
threats.hypothesis_description: ValidatorRegistry.totalUptimeScore() iterates over ALL validators for a virtualId in an unbounded loop. Combined with the permissionless addValidator (ECON-H003), an attacker can register thousands of dummy validators, making totalUptimeScore and any dependent functions revert due to block gas limits.
threats.investigation_rationale: The loop in totalUptimeScore calls validatorScore for each validator, which makes external calls to the DAO. Thousands of validators would easily exceed the block gas limit.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unbounded for loop over validatorCount(virtualId) calling validatorScore which involves external calls

preconditions: Target agent exists
totalUptimeScore is used by dependent protocol logic (rewards, ELO)
Attacker has gas for registering ~1000+ validators

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: totalUptimeScore iterates all validators with external call per iteration; gas cost grows linearly
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Register 5000+ unique addresses as validators via addValidator
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Denial of service on governance scoring and any reward distribution using totalUptimeScore

economic_questions: What functions depend on totalUptimeScore?
Can this permanently brick an agent's governance?
What is the cost of registering enough validators to exceed gas limit?
validation_requirements: Estimate gas per validatorScore call (includes DAO external call)
Calculate validators needed to exceed 30M gas block limit
Verify validators array grows unboundedly

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: ~0.5-2 ETH for gas on Base to register thousands of validators
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Griefing attack - no direct profit but disrupts agent operations

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All participants of the targeted agent
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Agent functionality and dependent reward systems

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test with 1000+ validators to confirm DoS

threats.title: Zero Slippage Protection on Tax Autoswap Enables Atomic Value Extraction
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentToken._swapTax passes amountOutMin=0 to swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens, providing zero slippage protection. An attacker can atomically manipulate the pool price and trigger autoswap within a single transaction, extracting tax revenue that should go to the project tax recipient.
threats.investigation_rationale: The autoswap is triggered during any non-LP transfer when accumulated tax exceeds the threshold. An attacker can craft a transaction that first manipulates pool reserves then triggers a transfer that causes autoswap at the manipulated price.

proof_of_signal.pattern: swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens called with amountOutMin=0; any output accepted

preconditions: Token has tax enabled (_tokenHasTax == true)
Accumulated tax balance >= swap threshold
Pool has sufficient liquidity for manipulation
Attacker can trigger autoswap via non-LP transfer in same tx as manipulation

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: amountOutMin = 0 means swap accepts any output, including near-zero after manipulation
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Atomic tx: manipulate pool reserves → transfer triggers autoswap at manipulated price → reverse manipulation
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Capture the difference between fair-value and manipulated-price swap output

economic_questions: What is the typical accumulated tax balance before autoswap triggers?
Can the attacker atomically manipulate price and trigger autoswap in one tx?
What pool depth is needed for profitable manipulation?
validation_requirements: Test atomic price manipulation + autoswap trigger
Calculate profit margins accounting for tax on attacker's trades
Verify _eligibleForSwap conditions during atomic attack

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Flash loan capital proportional to pool depth
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to accumulated tax balance (can be 100% extraction with 0 slippage)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Variable; up to 90%+ of accumulated tax value

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Shallow agent token pools are more exploitable
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right when tax balance reaches threshold

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All accumulated and future tax swaps

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Model atomic manipulation profit vs gas costs

threats.title: Tax Rate Above 100% Causes Unchecked Underflow Creating Phantom Token Supply
threats.hypothesis_description: setProjectTaxRates has no upper bound validation. If tax basis points are set >= BP_DENOM (10000), _taxProcessing computes tax > sentAmount, and the unchecked subtraction amountLessTax_ -= tax wraps to near uint256.max, crediting the recipient with astronomical token amounts and breaking the entire token economy.
threats.investigation_rationale: The entire _taxProcessing function runs in an unchecked block. Tax = sentAmount * basisPoints / 10000. If basisPoints >= 10001, tax > sentAmount, and the subtraction underflows.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Entire _taxProcessing in unchecked block; amountLessTax_ -= tax underflows if tax > sentAmount when BP > 10000

preconditions: Owner or factory calls setProjectTaxRates with values >= 10001
A buy or sell transaction occurs through a liquidity pool
_tokenHasTax == true and _autoSwapInProgress == false

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: If projectSellTaxBasisPoints = 10001: tax = sentAmount * 10001 / 10000 > sentAmount; amountLessTax_ = sentAmount - tax wraps to ~uint256.max
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001) then any pool trade creates near-infinite tokens for recipient
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Recipient receives ~uint256.max tokens, completely breaking token supply/price

economic_questions: What is the blast radius of a single underflowed transfer?
Can this be exploited to drain LP pools?
Does the _balances overflow propagate to other functions?
validation_requirements: Confirm uint16 storage allows values up to 65535 for tax rates
Test setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001) then execute a swap
Verify underflow produces near-max uint256 balance

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Complete token supply corruption - effectively infinite
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Two transactions: set rates + trigger trade

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: uint16 range allows up to 65535 (655% tax rate)
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test exact underflow behavior in unchecked context

threats.title: Tax Rate Monotonicity Not Enforced Despite Documented Constraint
threats.hypothesis_description: setProjectTaxRates comment states rates are 'subject to only ever decreasing' but the code has no enforcement. Owner/factory can increase tax rates to 100% (10000 BP) at any time, effectively trapping holders who can only sell at total loss. This is a code bug: the documented invariant is not implemented.
threats.investigation_rationale: Token holders rely on the stated property that tax rates can only decrease. The lack of enforcement allows rug-pull via tax rate manipulation. A launch at 2% tax attracting buyers, followed by increase to 99%, would devastate holders.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Comment says 'subject to only ever decreasing' but no check: newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectBuyTaxBasisPoints is missing

preconditions: Owner or factory calls setProjectTaxRates with higher values
Token has been launched with lower tax rates attracting holders

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No comparison between old and new rates; direct assignment: projectBuyTaxBasisPoints = newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Launch with 1% tax, attract holders, then set tax to 99%
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Tax recipient captures 99% of all sell transactions

economic_questions: How many tokens have been launched with this code?
What is the typical token TVL at risk?
Is there any external enforcement mechanism?
validation_requirements: Confirm no require/assert checking old >= new rates
Test setting rates from 100 to 5000 BP
Verify increased tax applies immediately to next trade

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 99% of all sell volume captured as tax
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Very high on accumulated sell volume

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Token price collapse as holders cannot sell without massive tax

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All token holders attempting to sell
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 99% loss on sell transactions
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total market cap of the token

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current tax rates across deployed tokens
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no external contract enforces monotonicity

threats.title: totalSupply uint128 Truncation Creates Supply Tracking Mismatch
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentToken._mint uses `_totalSupply += uint128(amount)` while `_balances[account] += amount` uses full uint256. The supply validation checks maxSupply <= type(uint128).max on raw units, but minting multiplies by 10^18. If lpSupply * 10^18 > type(uint128).max (possible when lpSupply > ~3.4e20), _totalSupply silently truncates while _balances remain accurate, breaking the conservation invariant.
threats.investigation_rationale: The check `maxSupply > type(uint128).max` validates the raw number, not the scaled amount. After multiplication by 10^18, the actual minted amount can exceed uint128, causing _totalSupply to record a wrong value while balances are correct. This can break any logic depending on totalSupply.
deployment_assumptions: Supply params must be set such that lpSupply * 10^18 or vaultSupply * 10^18 exceeds uint128 max
threats.config_evidence: AgentToken.sol initialize(): lpSupply = supplyParams.lpSupply * (10 ** decimals()); _mint uses _totalSupply += uint128(amount). Check is on raw maxSupply not scaled amount.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _totalSupply += uint128(amount) silently truncates; _balances[account] += amount uses uint256; _processSupplyParams checks maxSupply not maxSupply * 10^18

preconditions: Admin sets supply params where lpSupply or vaultSupply > ~3.4e20 raw units
Scaled amount (supply * 10^18) exceeds type(uint128).max

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _totalSupply += uint128(lpSupply * 1e18) truncates if lpSupply * 1e18 > 2^128; _balances += lpSupply * 1e18 is accurate
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set large supply params through factory; totalSupply() returns wrong value
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Broken totalSupply affects swap threshold calculation, tax distribution proportions, and external integrations

economic_questions: What supply values are used in production?
Can executeBondingCurveApplication set dangerous supply values?
What logic depends on totalSupply accuracy?
validation_requirements: Calculate exact threshold: supply * 1e18 > type(uint128).max
Test _mint with amount exceeding uint128
Verify _burn also uses uint128 cast (_totalSupply -= uint128(amount))

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Admin access to set supply params or BONDING_ROLE
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Dependent on how totalSupply mismatch is exploited

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All holders of the affected token

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current production supply params from deployment scripts
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Exact uint128 boundary for scaled amounts

threats.title: Sentinel Value Collision When _nextId Starts at 0 Enables Double Token Registration
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentFactoryV4 uses _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] == 0 as sentinel for 'no application exists'. If _nextId is initialized to 0, the first application gets ID 0, making _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] = 0 indistinguishable from 'not registered'. This allows the same token to be registered twice, and the second withdrawer can drain the factory's balance of that token.
threats.investigation_rationale: V4's initialize() accepts nextId_ parameter. If set to 0 (possible misconfiguration), the sentinel check breaks. V2 hardcodes _nextId=1, but V3/V4 accept it as parameter.
threats.config_evidence: AgentFactoryV4.sol initialize() takes nextId_ parameter; no check that nextId_ > 0. V2's initialize sets _nextId = 1 explicitly.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_tokenApplication[tokenAddr] == 0) uses 0 as sentinel; _nextId can be 0 making first application ID = 0

preconditions: Factory initialized with nextId_ = 0
First initFromToken creates application ID 0
Same token used in second initFromToken call

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _tokenApplication[token] = 0 (first app ID) == 0 (sentinel) → check passes for duplicate
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Register same token twice; withdraw both to drain factory balance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Second withdrawal drains factory's entire balance of that custom token

economic_questions: What nextId_ value is used in production deployment?
Can the first application be exploited before it's withdrawn?
What custom tokens might be affected?
validation_requirements: Check deployment scripts for nextId_ parameter
Test double registration with nextId_ = 0
Verify withdraw behavior for duplicate applications

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Factory's entire balance of the duplicated custom token
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High if factory holds significant balances

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When factory holds custom token balances

threats.title: Bot Protection Duration Stored But Never Enforced
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentToken stores botProtectionDurationInSeconds during initialization but never references it in transfer logic. There is no sniper/bot protection despite the parameter suggesting it exists. Bots can immediately trade after initial liquidity is added, sniping the launch.
threats.investigation_rationale: The parameter is set in initialize but grep of the codebase shows no usage in _transfer, _pretaxValidationAndLimits, or any other transfer-related function. This is dead code creating a false sense of security.
threats.config_evidence: Deployment script v2/deployMigrator.ts sets BOT_PROTECTION env var in supply params, indicating intent to use bot protection

proof_of_signal.pattern: botProtectionDurationInSeconds = supplyParams.botProtectionDurationInSeconds; never referenced in any transfer logic

preconditions: Token is newly launched with initial liquidity just added
Bot protection was expected to prevent early sniping

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: botProtectionDurationInSeconds is stored but never checked in _transfer or _pretaxValidationAndLimits
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Bot monitors addInitialLiquidity event, immediately buys tokens at initial price
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Sniper bots capture significant value from initial price appreciation

economic_questions: What bot protection was expected?
How much value do launch snipers typically capture?
Is there an external mechanism providing bot protection?
validation_requirements: Search entire codebase for botProtectionDurationInSeconds usage beyond storage
Confirm no time-based restrictions in transfer logic
Verify tokens can be bought in same block as addInitialLiquidity

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Variable - depends on initial liquidity size
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 5-50% of initial liquidity value from price sniping

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Initial price spike harms organic buyers

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Early retail buyers who buy after sniper
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Proportional to initial liquidity per token launch

threats.title: Admin Can Bypass Founder Maturity Lock via setMatureAt
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentVeToken.setMatureAt allows ADMIN_ROLE holders on agentNft to set matureAt to any value including 0 (past timestamp). This bypasses the founder's initial lock, allowing immediate withdrawal of initialLock LP tokens. The intended 10-year lock provides security for stakers, but admin can eliminate it instantly.
threats.investigation_rationale: The matureAt value has no monotonicity enforcement. Admin can reduce it to 0, enabling immediate founder withdrawal. Combined with founder rug-pulling LP, this destroys token price.
threats.config_evidence: v2/deployMigrator.ts grants ADMIN_ROLE to migrator contract; ADMIN_ROLE holders can call setMatureAt

proof_of_signal.pattern: setMatureAt(uint256 _matureAt) sets matureAt to any value; no check _matureAt >= existing matureAt

preconditions: Admin (ADMIN_ROLE on agentNft) colludes with founder
Founder has initialLock balance in veToken
matureAt is set to a past timestamp (e.g., 0)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: setMatureAt(0) → block.timestamp >= 0 is always true → founder can withdraw initialLock immediately
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin calls setMatureAt(0), founder calls withdraw(initialLock)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Founder dumps LP tokens, crashing agent token price; captures LP value

economic_questions: How much LP is typically locked as initialLock?
What is the impact on token price of full LP removal?
Is there additional governance protection against this?
validation_requirements: Confirm setMatureAt has no lower bound
Test withdraw after setMatureAt(0)
Verify LP removal impact on pool

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When token has accumulated significant value

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All veToken stakers and token holders
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Significant loss from token price crash
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire LP pool value and token market cap

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test setMatureAt with value < current matureAt

threats.title: AgentFactoryV2 Uses Unsafe IERC20.transfer Instead of SafeERC20
threats.hypothesis_description: AgentFactoryV2.executeApplication uses IERC20(assetToken).transfer(token, initialAmount) instead of safeTransfer. If assetToken is a non-standard ERC20 that returns false on failure rather than reverting, the transfer silently fails. The execution continues with zero pairToken in the AgentToken, creating a broken liquidity pool with only agent tokens and no pair tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: V3 and V4 correctly use SafeERC20.safeTransfer for the same operation. The V2 code path is inconsistent, suggesting a bug rather than intentional design.
deployment_assumptions: assetToken must be a non-standard ERC20 that returns false instead of reverting on failure
threats.config_evidence: Documentation states assetToken is VIRTUAL on Base. If VIRTUAL follows standard ERC20 (reverts on failure), this is not exploitable. V3/V4 use safeTransfer (AgentFactoryV3.sol:172, AgentFactoryV4.sol:158).

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20(assetToken).transfer(token, initialAmount) — raw transfer without SafeERC20 wrapper; V3/V4 use safeTransfer

preconditions: assetToken returns false on transfer failure instead of reverting
Transfer to agent token contract fails (e.g., insufficient balance, paused token)
AgentFactoryV2 is still in active use

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: IERC20.transfer return value unchecked; execution continues with zero pairToken deposited
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: If transfer fails silently, addInitialLiquidity creates pool with zero pairToken
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Broken LP pool created where agent tokens have no backing; all applicationThreshold funds lost

economic_questions: Is AgentFactoryV2 still deployed and in active use?
What is the VIRTUAL token's transfer failure behavior?
How many agents were created through V2?
validation_requirements: Check VIRTUAL token source for transfer return value behavior
Verify if V2 factory is still accepting applications
Test with mock token that returns false

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A - primarily a fund loss risk
